use std::thread;

fn fetch_data_async(url: &str) -> Result<String, Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
    let rt = tokio::runtime::Runtime::new()?;
    let response = rt.block_on(async {
        let resp = reqwest::get(url).await?;
        let body = resp.text().await?;
        Ok::<String, Box<dyn std::error::Error>>(body)
    })?;
    Ok(response)
}

fn process_in_background() {
    thread::spawn(|| {
        let rt = tokio::runtime::Runtime::new().unwrap();
        rt.block_on(async {
            let data = fetch_remote_data().await.unwrap();
            process_data(&data);
        });
    });
}

fn background_task(url: String) {
    thread::spawn(move || {
        let result = reqwest::blocking::get(&url).unwrap();
        let body = result.text().unwrap();
        let _ = tokio::runtime::Runtime::new().unwrap().block_on(async {
            save_to_db(&body).await
        });
    });
}

async fn fetch_remote_data() -> Result<String, Box<dyn std::error::Error>> { Ok("data".into()) }
fn process_data(_data: &str) {}
async fn save_to_db(_data: &str) -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> { Ok(()) }
